label: CEMA Business Climate
explanation: Monthly survey of European agricultural machinery manufacturers measuring business mood and 6-month outlook. Scale: -100 (deep recession) to +100 (strong boom). Values below 0 indicate recession territory.
yoyComparison: Jun 2025: approx. +5 | Jun 2026: -20 | Change: -25 pts YoY

label: Equipment Demand Outlook
explanation: Forward-looking component of the CEMA Barometer. Manufacturers report expected turnover for the next 6 months. Currently negative, indicating expected demand contraction for agricultural machinery and parts.
yoyComparison: Jun 2025: Slightly positive | Jun 2026: Negative | Sentiment reversed

label: Construction PMI
explanation: S&P Global Eurozone Construction PMI measures purchasing managers sentiment. 50 = no change. Below 50 = contraction. The construction sector has been in contraction for over 4 years.

label: Brent Crude
explanation: Brent crude oil benchmark price. Lower oil prices reduce fuel costs for farmers and construction operators, positively affecting equipment utilization and parts demand.

label: EU Agri-Equipment Exports
explanation: Year-over-year growth of EU agricultural equipment exports to EMEA region. Strong export growth indicates expanding addressable market for aftermarket spare parts.
yoyComparison: Q1 2025: +9% YoY | Q1 2026: +15% YoY | Export momentum accelerating

label: ECB Rate Outlook
explanation: ECB monetary policy direction. Rate cuts reduce financing costs for dealers and end customers, unlocking pent-up capex on equipment.
yoyComparison: Jun 2025: 3.75% rate | Jun 2026: 3.0% rate, further cuts signaled

label: F&L Sector Health
explanation: No public F&L PMI exists. Husqvarna Group (largest listed forest & landscaping pure-play) quarterly organic growth serves as the best available proxy for sector health.
